description: Fix WebSocket.prototype.send being permanently patched with no teardown: the ClickUp plugin intercepts WebSocket.prototype.send to capture session tokens but never restores the original method.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/clickup && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Restore WebSocket.prototype.send in teardown() when the ClickUp adapter is deactivated
userStories.description: In plugins/clickup/src/clickup-api.ts, installWsInterceptor() overwrites WebSocket.prototype.send at module load (line 66 calls it immediately), but the OrigSend reference is scoped inside the function and there is no teardown(). The global WebSocket prototype stays patched for the tab's lifetime. Save OrigSend at module level and add a teardown() method to the plugin class that restores it.
acceptanceCriteria: After teardown(), WebSocket.prototype.send is the original browser method
The __cu_ws_interceptor_installed flag is reset to false in teardown() so re-injection works correctly
The ClickUpPlugin class in index.ts implements a teardown() method that restores the original send
